title: Architect the Manuscript
subtitle: A structural template for acts, scene dependencies, continuity risk, and the tools that keep the manuscript coherent.
scope: One manuscript where pacing, causality, and continuity need to be controlled explicitly.
sourceCoverage: Template only; replace placeholder act notes, scene links, continuity risks, and tooling references with real manuscript assets.

templateMeta.useWhen: Use this when the manuscript has enough moving parts that pacing, setup/payoff logic, and continuity risks need their own structural view.
templateMeta.operatingThesis: Treat manuscript architecture as a structural system: acts create the structural sequence, scenes carry setup/payoff obligations, and continuity risks should stay explicit before prose polish.
notFor: theme or storyline pressure mapping as the primary lens
general manuscript notes without dependency structure

semanticDefinition.objectiveRole: Make manuscript structure operational by linking acts, scene dependencies, continuity risks, and tooling.
semanticDefinition.templateSubtitle: A structural template for acts, scene dependencies, continuity risk, and the tools that keep the manuscript coherent.
semanticDefinition.templateScope: One manuscript where pacing, causality, and continuity need to be controlled explicitly.
semanticDefinition.templateObjective: Make the manuscript structurally defensible by surfacing major acts, scene dependencies, continuity risks, and the tools used to inspect them.
semanticDefinition.templateSuccessCondition: A reader can see what each act is doing, which scenes depend on earlier setup, and what continuity risks still threaten the manuscript.

sections.title: Status Snapshot
sections.role: Summarize manuscript architecture pressure without carrying the structural evidence.
sections.rationale: This section compresses manuscript readiness while acts, dependencies, continuity risks, and tooling remain inspectable in their own layers.

sections.title: Act / Part Structure
sections.role: Hold the ordered act or part structure that defines the manuscript path.
sections.rationale: The manuscript needs an ordered outline before dependencies and continuity risks can be located meaningfully.

sections.title: Scene Dependency Map
sections.role: Map setup and payoff dependencies between scenes.
sections.rationale: Scene relationships need edge-table shape because causality lives between setup and dependent scenes rather than inside one card.

sections.title: Continuity Watchlist
sections.role: Track unresolved contradictions and continuity risks across scenes, characters, and storylines.
sections.rationale: Continuity risks must stay visible until checked because unresolved contradictions can invalidate manuscript structure.

sections.title: Tooling Surface
sections.role: Expose the tools used to inspect, verify, and evolve the manuscript structure.
sections.rationale: Structural and continuity claims need tool paths so the manuscript can be rechecked instead of reread from scratch.

relationships.rationale: Manuscript status should summarize the actual act structure being controlled.
Scene dependencies are meaningful only against the manuscript sequence and act structure they support.
Setup/payoff dependencies expose the continuity risks that need to remain visible until checked.
Continuity checks need an operational tool path so the watchlist can be verified or refreshed.

stageSignals.when: content-outline has no act or part cards
stageSignals.question: What first act, part, or structural unit makes this manuscript tangible?

stageSignals.when: act structure exists but scene-dependency-map has no dependency rows
stageSignals.question: Which scene setup or payoff dependency should be made explicit?

stageSignals.when: scene dependencies exist but continuity-watchlist has no risks or checks
stageSignals.question: Which continuity risk follows from the current scene dependencies?

stageSignals.when: act structure, dependencies, continuity, and tooling are all populated and linked
stageSignals.question: Is the manuscript structurally defensible, or does a dependency or continuity risk still control the next pass?

validOperations.label: Add act structure card
validOperations.description: Add an act, part, or structural unit with source references and notes.

validOperations.label: Add scene dependency
validOperations.description: Add a setup-to-dependent scene relationship that makes causality explicit.

validOperations.label: Add continuity risk
validOperations.description: Add a continuity risk tied to scenes, characters, or storylines.

validOperations.label: Link tooling to continuity
validOperations.description: Link a script, workflow, or artifact used to inspect manuscript structure or continuity.

objective: Make the manuscript structurally defensible by surfacing major acts, scene dependencies, continuity risks, and the tools used to inspect them.
successCondition: A reader can see what each act is doing, which scenes depend on earlier setup, and what continuity risks still threaten the manuscript.

callouts.title: How to use this template
items: Use this when the draft exists enough that structural failure matters more than raw output volume.
Keep the dependency map factual and the continuity watchlist risk-based. Do not collapse them into one section.

sections.title: Status Snapshot

callout.title: Template intent
items: This template is for keeping the manuscript coherent under pressure.
If you mostly need to know what to draft next, stay in the simpler starter.

sections.rationale: This section compresses manuscript readiness while acts, dependencies, continuity risks, and tooling remain inspectable in their own layers.

sections.title: Act / Part Structure

data.name: Act I: Arrival
notes: Arrival, displacement, and the first evidence that the house has been curated before Lena came back.

data.name: Act II: Search
notes: The archive line and the village debt line should braid rather than alternate mechanically.

data.name: Act III: Reckoning
notes: Final structure exists conceptually, but the exact order of revelation and emotional settlement still needs testing.

sections.rationale: The manuscript needs an ordered outline before dependencies and continuity risks can be located meaningfully.

sections.title: Scene Dependency Map

notes: The opening establishes the household unease that makes the kitchen-table scene land.
The confrontation depends on the reader already understanding that the archive disappearance was intentional.
The family-debt pressure is present, but the specific trigger that turns private resentment into open accusation still needs a cleaner causal bridge.

sections.rationale: Scene relationships need edge-table shape because causality lives between setup and dependent scenes rather than inside one card.

sections.title: Continuity Watchlist

data.name: Boat arrival timeline
data.riskSummary: The manuscript currently implies conflicting travel times between Lena's arrival and Tomas reaching the house.
data.nextCheck: Validate the timeline against the chapter-outline clock before revising the archive-room scene.
evidence: Archive-room outline currently assumes Tomas has already completed an errand that would take longer than the gap allows.

data.name: Mother's knowledge boundary
data.riskSummary: It is not yet clear exactly how much the mother knows about the archive removal before the confrontation.
data.nextCheck: Mark every sentence that implies prior knowledge and decide whether she is withholding facts or discovering them in real time.
evidence: Kitchen-table scene hints at prior awareness.
Confrontation draft currently swings between denial and explanation without a firm boundary.

sections.rationale: Continuity risks must stay visible until checked because unresolved contradictions can invalidate manuscript structure.

sections.title: Tooling Surface

data.name: Universal renderer
data.purpose: Render the manuscript structure into a portable HTML snapshot that preserves acts, dependencies, and watchlists together.
data.whenToUse: Use when you want to review manuscript architecture without keeping the raw JSON open.
caveats: The rendered snapshot is shareable, but the JSON stays authoritative.

data.name: Timeline sheet
data.purpose: Keep event order, travel durations, and same-day constraints visible while revising scene dependencies.
data.whenToUse: Use whenever a scene depends on timing, causality, or off-page actions.
caveats: A timeline sheet catches chronology problems, but it does not automatically expose emotional or thematic discontinuity.

sections.rationale: Structural and continuity claims need tool paths so the manuscript can be rechecked instead of reread from scratch.
